title: Shakshuka
tagline: Eggs poached in spiced tomato sauce. One skillet, all the flavor.
description: A North African and Middle Eastern classic—eggs cooked in a rich, spiced tomato and pepper sauce. Served with crusty bread for dipping.
ingredients: Fresh cilantro or parsley for serving
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper. Sauté 5-7 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, cumin, paprika, and cayenne. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in crushed tomatoes, salt, and pepper. Simmer 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Make 6 wells in the sauce. Crack an egg into each well.
Cover and cook 5-8 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Top with feta and fresh herbs. Serve immediately from the skillet with crusty bread.
notes: Yolk doneness: 5-6 minutes for runny, 8-10 for set. Cover traps steam and cooks tops of eggs.
Spice level: Cayenne is optional. Harissa paste is a more authentic addition.
Add-ins: Chorizo, merguez, or crispy chickpeas for protein.
Feta: Not traditional but common. Goat cheese works too.
The bread: Non-negotiable. You need something to soak up the sauce.
